points: New York is the epicenter of BESS restrictions.

As of the June 2026 tracker refresh, New York had over 100 active local BESS moratoriums — more than 80% of every active BESS moratorium in the U.S. Westchester remains among the most-restricted counties. The coalition tracks 5 towns with an active moratorium (Bedford, Cortlandt, New Castle, the Village of Ossining, and Pound Ridge); Cortlandt's moratorium runs through Dec 31, 2026 per LL 7-2025, and Pound Ridge's through Sept 24, 2026. Mamaroneck Village concluded its moratorium drafting period and adopted a permanent restrictive code (LL 7-2026: Tier 1 ≤40/80 kWh; >80 kWh prohibited). Several towns have outright bans (Mount Pleasant, Mount Kisco, Somers).
Local control is decisive for standalone projects.

The State Office of Renewable Energy Siting (ORES) has no jurisdiction over standalone battery storage. A standalone BESS of any size must be permitted locally, so an active town moratorium or ban is binding with no state workaround. Only storage co-located with a solar/wind project ≥25 MW can use ORES to override local zoning.

Senate Bill S5506 (Kavanagh) and Assembly Bill A8378 (Levenberg) would expand ORES to standalone storage. Introduced Feb 2025, still in the Energy & Telecommunications Committee as of June 2026, not passed either chamber. Several Westchester municipalities (e.g., Yorktown) passed resolutions opposing it.
Most moratoriums are temporary — but some harden into bans.

Moratoriums are usually a pause while a town writes zoning; a large NY cluster expires mid-to-late 2026. In Westchester, Mount Pleasant and Mount Kisco have moved from moratorium to effective bans, while Port Chester adopted permanent regulations instead. The next several months are the highest-leverage window to shape the permanent rules.
links: NYSERDA — NY BESS Guidebook & Model Law

towns.name: Town of Bedford
towns.scope: Tier 1 (<80 kWh) allowed; large-scale moratorium until 8/17/26
towns.subtitle: Northern Westchester — Bedford Hills, Katonah, Bedford Village
snapshot: Moratorium adopted Aug 13, 2024; extended ~6 months — was set to expire July 16, 2025, extended through Jan 16, 2026 (confirm any further extension).
Concerns after fires at three NY BESS facilities since May 2023 and the volatility of lithium-ion batteries, especially large-scale.
HIGH — moratorium time is being used to study and draft rules.
summary: Bedford adopted a BESS moratorium on Aug 13, 2024 and extended it (originally to expire July 16, 2025) through January 16, 2026 while it studies safety and prepares regulations; listed ACTIVE on the tracker.
The moratorium supersedes the usual Town/Planning/Zoning Board application timelines. Engage during this drafting window.
Local law: LL 1 of 2026: Tier 1 < 80 kWh allowed; Tier 2 > 80 kWh under a large-scale moratorium until 8/17/2026. (per the workgroup GIS sheet).
flags: Bedford remains ACTIVE on the Carina tracker (refreshed June 14, 2026), so the moratorium was extended past Jan 16, 2026 — but the current expiration date is unconfirmed. The full Town Board roster / a direct phone weren't verified. Confirm via the Town Board page and Agenda Center.
committees: Town Board — adopted and extends the moratorium.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — future permitting.
Conservation Board / sustainability committees — likely allies (confirm names).
meetings: Schedule: Town Board meets the 1st & 3rd Tuesdays at the Town House (321 Bedford Road, Bedford Hills); public open sessions at the start and end; Zoom available.

officials.name: 5-member board incl. Bobbi Bittker and Andres L. Castillo (confirm full roster)

towns.name: Town of Cortlandt (Cortlandt Manor)
towns.scope: Active moratorium — all BESS (expires 12/31/26 per LL 7-2025); code prohibits Tier 3
towns.subtitle: Northwestern Westchester — Hudson River to the Putnam County line
snapshot: LL 2-2024 (original) → LL 10-2024 → LL 2-2025 (through Dec 31, 2025) → LL 7-2025 (through Dec 31, 2026, per the Dec 9, 2025 public hearing notice). Active moratorium.
Significant developer interest; town wants time to consult experts and await State/County guidance before adopting siting rules.
HIGH — a committee is actively drafting the permanent local law.
summary: Cortlandt has had a moratorium in place continuously since 2024. After strong developer interest, the Town Board enacted LL 2-2024, convened a committee to draft a permanent law, and extended via LL 10-2024 and LL 2 of 2025 (through Dec 31, 2025). Still ACTIVE in 2026.
Because the town is openly drafting, this is one of the best chances to influence the actual ordinance — setbacks, fire/safety standards, permitted zones.

Local law: LL 7-2025 extends the moratorium on all BESS through 12/31/2026 (confirmed via the Town Board's Dec 9, 2025 public hearing notice; note that the workgroup GIS sheet notation 'until 07-2026' appears to be a date-entry issue — the legal notice text controls). The default code prohibits Tier 3 (utility-scale) systems.
flags: LL 7-2025 confirmed to extend the moratorium through Dec 31, 2026, per the Town Board's public hearing notice for the December 9, 2025 meeting (fetched 6/30/26). The workgroup GIS sheet notes 'until 07-2026,' but the legal notice text stating 'through the end of 2026' is authoritative. Confirm LL 7-2025 was formally adopted at the Dec 9, 2025 meeting via the Town Clerk.
committees: Green Team / Climate Smart Task Force — sustainability body; natural ally.
Town Board BESS drafting committee — writing the permanent law (reach via Supervisor's office).
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — review future BESS special permits.
meetings: Schedule: Town Board meets monthly on a Tuesday at 7:00 PM, Vincent F. Nyberg General Meeting Room, Town Hall (1 Heady Street). Three Monday work sessions/month. Open to public.
Upcoming 2026 (7:00 PM): Jul 21, Aug 11, Sep 15, Oct 20, Nov 17, Dec 8.

officials.name: James F. Creighton, Cristin Jacoby, Robert E. Mayes, Joyce C. White

towns.name: Town of New Castle (Chappaqua / Millwood)
towns.scope: Moratorium lapsed 5/31/26; tiered law proposed (Tier 1 40/80, Tier 2 ≤600, Tier 3 >600 kWh) — not yet adopted
towns.subtitle: Central Westchester
snapshot: 50% Red, 50% Yellow — Moderate capacity (ConEd/NYSEG hosting-capacity map)

A draft permanent BESS local law would allow Tier 1 (≤80 kWh) only and explicitly prohibit Tier 2 and Tier 3 — stricter than the tiered Tier 1/2/3 framework previously described here. Referred to the Planning Board, which issued its response March 4, 2026; not yet shown as adopted by the Town Board.
Supervisor Tipp cited NY's 70%-by-2030 / 100%-by-2040 goals alongside local protections after BESS fires elsewhere.
HIGH — permanent law is drafted and pending a Town Board vote; this is the window to push back on the Tier 2/3 prohibition.
summary: New Castle paused battery-storage (and large-scale solar) development while it writes siting rules. Supervisor Victoria Tipp framed it as balancing NY's clean-energy targets against safety concerns from BESS fires elsewhere. Per Carina Energy's June 2026 tracker, the moratorium remains active through roughly August 2026.

The permanent law under consideration has shifted from a permissive tiered framework toward one that would bar Tier 2 and Tier 3 storage entirely (Tier 1 ≤80 kWh only) — a meaningfully stricter direction than earlier drafts. The Planning Board responded to the referral March 4, 2026; the Town Board has not yet voted. Real room to influence the final thresholds before adoption.

Local law: Moratorium on BESS / large-scale solar (LL 1-2025, extended by LL 7-2025); the workgroup's spreadsheet shows the extension chain running through ~5/31/2026, but Carina Energy's June 2026 statewide update lists New Castle as ACTIVE through approximately August 2026 — likely a further extension not yet posted to the Town's online Approved Legislation page (checked 6/29/26, no 2026 entries listed yet). (per the workgroup GIS sheet).
flags: Confirm (1) the full Town Board roster + member emails — the site is JavaScript-driven, so only Tipp, Saland, Levin, and Morton were verifiable — and (2) the exact current expiration date: the workgroup spreadsheet implies the original extension chain ran only through 5/31/2026, but Carina's June 2026 update says ACTIVE through ~Aug 2026 and the Town's Approved Legislation page shows no 2026 entries yet — there is a real possibility of a legal gap if no further extension was actually adopted. Verify directly with the Town Clerk before relying on either source.
Confirm whether the Town Board has voted on the proposed permanent law (Tier 1 only, Tier 2/3 prohibited) since the Planning Board's March 4, 2026 referral response.
The six-month moratorium lapsed 5/31/26; the proposed tiered law is not yet adopted — an open window to shape the code.
committees: Town Board — leads the moratorium and the eventual regulation.
Sustainability Advisory Board / climate committees — coordinate for a pro-clean-energy framing.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — future permitting authorities.
meetings: Schedule & agendas: Town Board meets at New Castle Town Hall, 200 South Greeley Avenue, Chappaqua. See the Town Board page for the current calendar, agendas, roster and emails.

officials.name: include Lauren Levin and Lori Morton (confirm full roster + emails)

towns.name: Village of Ossining
towns.scope: Active moratorium — all BESS; Local Law 4-2026, an 8-month term from April 2026
towns.subtitle: Western Westchester — Hudson River
summary: Local Law 4-2026: moratorium on all BESS, an 8-month term effective ~4/22/2026 — implying an expiration around late December 2026 (computed estimate, not independently confirmed). (Verified in the workgroup GIS sheet.)
flags: The ~late-December-2026 expiration is computed from the workgroup's '8-month moratorium from 04-2026' note, not from an independently verified end date — confirm the exact adoption date and term in Local Law 4-2026's text before treating this as firm.

towns.name: Town of Mount Pleasant
towns.subtitle: Central Westchester — Valhalla, Thornwood, Hawthorne
snapshot: 50% Orange, 50% Yellow — Moderate capacity (ConEd/NYSEG hosting-capacity map)
Local law (Dec 2025) permanently bans the siting/construction of new BESS — moving from a Sept 2024–Sept 2025 moratorium to a ban. Effective immediately.
Lithium-ion fire risk; the Board cited the July 2025 US EPA BESS safety guidance and proximity to the Saw Mill River, wetlands, schools, and the Westchester Medical Center complex.
LOW — ban in place; engagement means monitoring for reconsideration or legal challenge.
summary: Mount Pleasant moved from a moratorium to a permanent ban on new BESS in December 2025. Supervisor Carl Fulgenzi said the decision followed considerable public input and study of BESS mishaps nationwide; the Board referenced the July 2025 US EPA safety guidance.
As one of the hardest-line jurisdictions in the county, Mount Pleasant is mainly a monitor-and-watch town unless the Board revisits the ban.
Local law: Town Code §218-20.7: BESS prohibited from being sited anywhere in the Town. (per the workgroup GIS sheet).
flags: Status shown as a permanent ban per the town's Dec 2025 prohibition local law and press release. The Carina tracker (refreshed June 26, 2026) now lists Mount Pleasant's moratorium as LIFTED (type BAN) — consistent with the moratorium having been replaced by the adopted prohibition law, which governs. Bans can be challenged or revised; track any reconsideration, variance pathway, or litigation.
committees: Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — would handle any future change.
meetings: Schedule & agendas: Town Board agendas and the meeting calendar are posted on the town site; confirm current dates there.

officials.name: Danielle Zaino (Deputy Supervisor), Mark Saracino, Joe Bonanno, Thomas Sialiano
One Town Hall Plaza, Valhalla, NY 10595

towns.name: Town of Somers
towns.scope: Ban — BESS prohibited via solar code
towns.subtitle: Northern Westchester — rural; Lincolndale, Somers hamlet, Granite Springs
snapshot: NYSEG — NYSEG territory (not on the ConEd map) (ConEd/NYSEG hosting-capacity map)
Town amended its code to ban large-scale battery farms after a 116 MW BESS proposal (East Point Energy, Miller Road, near the Mahopac border) was withdrawn in early 2025.
Strong resident opposition (Somers and neighboring Mahopac) to a large utility-scale facility near the town line.
LOW for large-scale — code now bans big battery farms; treatment of smaller systems should be confirmed.
summary: After East Point Energy withdrew its 116-megawatt battery facility planned on Miller Road near the Somers/Mahopac border in early 2025, the Town Board amended its code to ban battery farms of that size, propelled by an active resident movement spanning Somers and Mahopac.
Somers is included as a relevant Westchester jurisdiction; confirm the exact size thresholds and whether smaller BESS remain permitted.
Local law: 2025 §170-80 (Solar Energy) bans incorporation of BESS. (per the workgroup GIS sheet).
flags: The size threshold and the amended code section should be confirmed from the Town of Somers code/minutes; this entry is based on news reporting, not the full local law.
meetings: Confirm the Town Board meeting schedule and agendas on the Town of Somers official website.

officials.name: Town of Somers — confirm Supervisor and Town Board on the official site

hosting.tier: NYSEG territory (not on the ConEd map)

towns.name: Village/Town of Mount Kisco
towns.scope: Ban — all BESS prohibited, any size (≈Aug 2026)
towns.subtitle: Northern Westchester
snapshot: Aug 2025: Village Board voted unanimously not to classify BESS as a 'public utility,' effectively prohibiting new BESS village-wide — after twice extending a 2024 moratorium.
Mayor Cindrich said the Board wanted to protect the village until the technology improves and firefighters are better equipped for lithium-ion fires.
Mount Kisco already hosts three large-scale BESS facilities (Oakwood Cemetery/former landfill, courthouse/police roof) plus smaller units.
summary: Mount Kisco enacted a six-month moratorium in 2024 (with two three-month extensions) after a large-scale BESS was proposed near the Diamond Properties soccer field at 333 North Bedford Road. In August 2025 the Village Board effectively banned new BESS by declining to treat them as a 'public utility' under the village code.
Notably, several BESS facilities already operate in the village, so the ban applies to new development.
Local law: BESS of any size is not an allowed use in any district in the Village. (per the workgroup GIS sheet).
flags: The ban was implemented via a zoning interpretation (BESS not a 'public utility') rather than a standalone ban law; confirm the exact mechanism and any challenge. The Carina tracker (refreshed June 14, 2026) lists Mount Kisco's earlier moratorium as EXPIRED — consistent with the Village replacing the moratorium with the Aug 2025 effective ban.
committees: Village Board of Trustees — adopted the effective ban.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — would handle any future change.
meetings: Schedule & agendas: Confirm the Village Board meeting schedule and agendas on the Mount Kisco site.
links: The Examiner — Mount Kisco BESS moratorium

towns.name: Town of Greenburgh
towns.subtitle: Central Westchester — six villages + unincorporated area
snapshot: 75% Yellow, 25% Orange — Moderate capacity (ConEd/NYSEG hosting-capacity map)
Already has a BESS law; the Town is considering amendments as part of broader land-use code changes.
Amendments were referred to the Planning Board for review and recommendation over several months — an open process to influence.
summary: Unlike the moratorium towns, Greenburgh already has a BESS law and is now revising it as part of a broader package of land-use code amendments. The Town Board referred the changes to the Planning Board, which will hear staff presentations and discuss them over several months.
Because the rules are being actively reworked, the workgroup can engage on the substance of the amendments at Town Board and Planning Board meetings.
Local law: LL 5-2021 (Ch 285 Zoning): Tier 1 ≤80 kWh, Tier 2 ≤6,000 kWh, Tier 3 ≤80 MWh. Amendments currently in progress. (per the workgroup GIS sheet).
flags: The specifics of the existing BESS law and the proposed amendments should be reviewed directly; this town was added as a relevant non-moratorium jurisdiction. Confirm the Town Board roster/contacts.
committees: Town Board — 4 Council members + Supervisor; initiates code amendments.
Planning Board — currently reviewing the BESS amendments.
Greenburgh Nature Center / sustainability bodies — potential allies.
meetings: Schedule: Town Board meets the 2nd & 4th Wednesday at 7:30 PM at Greenburgh Town Hall (177 Hillside Avenue); in person or Zoom.
towns.drReason: Adopted a complete, balanced three-tier BESS code (small / medium / utility-scale) back in 2021 — a full framework other towns can copy, now being refreshed.

towns.name: Village of Briarcliff Manor
towns.subtitle: Northern Westchester
snapshot: 75% Red, 25% Yellow — Low capacity (ConEd/NYSEG hosting-capacity map)
summary: Adopted June 2025 (Ch 178 Art II): Tier 1 to 600 kWh; prohibits Tier 2 > 600 kWh. (Verified in the workgroup GIS sheet.)
flags: Over-strict: Prohibits Tier 2 (>600 kWh) — blocks utility-scale storage. An engagement target to loosen toward a Croton-style tiered code.

towns.name: Village of Croton-on-Hudson
towns.subtitle: Northwestern Westchester — Hudson River (within Cortlandt)
summary: Adopted May 2024 (Ch 230 Definitions): Tier 1 to 200 kWh; Tier 2 over 200 kWh. Already permitting real projects (e.g., a 280 kWh system at Croton Auto Park). (Verified in the workgroup GIS sheet.)
towns.drReason: The flagship 'doing it right' case: a clear, adopted tiered code (2024) that permits storage with sensible size thresholds, and is actively used to approve real projects.

towns.name: Village of Larchmont
towns.scope: Restrictive — Tier 1 ≤600 kWh; Tier 2 (>600 kWh) prohibited
towns.subtitle: Southeastern Westchester — Long Island Sound
summary: LL 1-2026 (§100-6): Tier 1 to 600 kWh permitting; Tier 2 over 600 kWh. (Verified in the workgroup GIS sheet.)
flags: Over-strict: LL 2-2026 prohibits Tier 2 (>600 kWh). An engagement target to loosen toward a Croton-style tiered code.

towns.name: Village of Port Chester
towns.subtitle: Southeastern Westchester — Long Island Sound, NY/CT border
snapshot: Ended its moratorium in early 2025 and adopted permanent BESS regulations amending Chapter 345 (Zoning), after a SEQRA negative declaration.
Prompted partly by a BESS application on North Main Street; the Village chose to regulate rather than ban or keep pausing.
MODERATE — rules exist; engage on amendments or specific applications.
summary: Port Chester moved from a temporary moratorium to permanent BESS zoning regulations in early 2025 (amending Chapter 345), adopting a SEQRA negative declaration in the process. The trigger was a pending BESS application on North Main Street.
Because Port Chester now allows BESS under defined standards, it is a useful reference model — and a place to engage on how those standards are applied or amended.
Local law: §345-1003: Tier 1 < 300 kWh, Tier 2 > 300 kWh (amended 4/26). Adopted permanent regulations after lifting its moratorium. (per the workgroup GIS sheet).
flags: The Mayor's full name and the specifics of the adopted BESS standards should be confirmed from the adopted local law / Chapter 345.
committees: Board of Trustees — Mayor + Trustees; adopted the regulations.
Planning Commission / Zoning Board of Appeals — apply the BESS standards.
meetings: Schedule & agendas: Board of Trustees agendas are posted on the Village Agenda Center; confirm the current meeting schedule there.

officials.name: 222 Grace Church Street, Port Chester, NY 10573

towns.drReason: Chose to write permanent, tiered zoning rules instead of banning or extending a moratorium — a clean post-moratorium model.

towns.name: City of Mount Vernon
towns.scope: Allowed via solar code (no standalone BESS law)
towns.subtitle: Southern Westchester — city bordering the Bronx
snapshot: No BESS moratorium, ban, or dedicated BESS law identified as of the May 2026 statewide tracker.
summary: Included for completeness. Does not appear in the statewide BESS moratorium tracker (May 2026); no dedicated city BESS law was identified. Not individually researched in depth — land-use authority rests with the City Council, Planning Board, and Zoning Board of Appeals.
Local law: Sec 224-A(9): BESS allowed under the solar code. (per the workgroup GIS sheet).
flags: Reference entry — not individually researched. Confirm current BESS status, meeting schedule, and officials on the city's official site.
Big-city context — permits BESS through normal site-plan/permitting rather than a special code; deprioritized for code advocacy.
meetings: Confirm the City Council meeting schedule and agendas on the city's official website.

towns.name: City of Peekskill
towns.scope: Tier 1 (<80 kWh) allowed; Tier 2 moratorium lapsed 5/31/26 (confirm)
towns.subtitle: Northwestern Westchester — Hudson River city
snapshot: Moratorium on lithium-ion BESS over 80 kW enacted May 2025 (unanimous); extended in late 2025.
City Attorney cited the volatile nature of lithium-ion batteries and the need for fire-suppression and ground/water-impact standards.
HIGH — city is studying and drafting BESS regulations.
summary: Peekskill enacted a moratorium on lithium-ion BESS over 80 kW in May 2025 (passed unanimously after a public hearing) and extended it in late 2025 while it studies safety and appropriate standards.
As a city with a Common Council, Peekskill's process differs from the towns — engagement runs through the Mayor and Council.
Local law: LL 14-2025: Tier 1 < 80 kWh allowed; the moratorium on Tier 2 > 80 kWh ran until 5/31/2026 and has now lapsed (confirm next step). (per the workgroup GIS sheet).
flags: Confirm the current expiration date and the six Common Council members' names/contacts on the city site.
Over-strict: Tier 1 (<80 kWh) only; the Tier 2 moratorium lapsed 5/31/26 with no Tier 2 pathway yet. An engagement target to loosen toward a Croton-style tiered code.

Carina Energy's June 2026 statewide update lists Peekskill as ACTIVE (not lapsed) alongside Bedford, Cortlandt, New Castle, and Yonkers — in tension with the 5/31/26 lapse date above. Re-verify with the City Clerk before relying on either source.
committees: Mayor & Common Council — the legislative body for any BESS law.
Planning Commission / Zoning Board of Appeals — permitting bodies.
meetings: Schedule: Common Council meets Mondays — a Committee of the Whole work session followed by the Common Council meeting (evening sessions typically 7:00 PM work session / 7:30 PM meeting).

snapshot: No BESS moratorium, ban, or dedicated BESS law identified as of the May 2026 statewide tracker.

flags: Reference entry — not individually researched. Confirm current BESS status, meeting schedule, and officials on the city's official site.

meetings: Confirm the City Council meeting schedule and agendas on the city's official website.

towns.name: Town / Village of Harrison
towns.scope: Allowed — Tier 1 >80 kWh, Tier 2 ≤600 kWh, Tier 3 >600 kWh by special permit (§235-95)
towns.subtitle: Central Westchester — coterminous Town and Village government
snapshot: LL 1 of 2025 — a 6-month moratorium effective March 6, 2025; lapsed ~September 2025 (Town Code Article XVII), succeeded by the tiered BESS standards now codified at §235-95.
Town Board, Planning Board and ZBA wanted more on fire suppression, emergency response, gas emissions, groundwater, and siting standards.
MEDIUM — no active pause; watch for a permanent BESS code and weigh in before it is finalized.
summary: Harrison adopted a six-month moratorium (LL 1-2025) effective March 6, 2025. It expired ~6 months later absent a Town Board extension, and the statewide tracker now lists Harrison as EXPIRED — no active pause on BESS applications.

BESS is now governed by Town Code §235-95, a tiered standard: Tier 1 (≤80 kWh) and Tier 2 (≤600 kWh) are permitted outright, while Tier 3 (>600 kWh, utility-scale) requires a Special Permit Use. This is a reasonably permissive model — engagement now should focus on the Special Permit Use criteria for Tier 3 (siting, fire suppression, decommissioning) rather than asking for tiered standards to be created from scratch.

Local law: The 2025 moratorium expired; BESS is now allowed via Town Code §235-95: Tier 1 ≤80 kWh and Tier 2 ≤600 kWh permitted, Tier 3 >600 kWh permitted with a Special Permit Use. (per the workgroup GIS sheet).
flags: Councilmember emails follow the town's standard format (firstinitiallastname@harrison-ny.gov) from a directory listing; not each confirmed on the official board page. Verify before formal outreach.
committees: Town / Village Board — adopted, and would extend or replace, the moratorium.
Planning Board — reviews site plans/special permits; cited in the rationale.
Zoning Board of Appeals — also cited as needing more BESS information.
meetings: Schedule: Town/Village Board meets twice monthly — typically 1st & 3rd Thursday at 7:00 PM (Sep–May) — and once monthly (1st Thursday, 7:00 PM) in Feb, Jul, Aug.
Location: Alfred F. Sulla, Jr. Municipal Building, 1 Heineman Place, Harrison, NY 10528.

snapshot: NYSEG — NYSEG territory (not on the ConEd map) (ConEd/NYSEG hosting-capacity map)
ALL battery storage, any size — one of only ~10 such jurisdictions in NY.

Six-month moratorium passed unanimously after a public hearing; a 2nd-extension public hearing was noticed for March 24, 2026 (per the Town Clerk's published notice), but Carina Energy's June 2026 statewide update lists Pound Ridge's moratorium as LIFTED rather than further extended.
Concerns about fires, hazardous-material leaks and incidents nationally — adopted even though no BESS facilities exist in town.
summary: Pound Ridge's moratorium covered ALL battery storage regardless of size (only ~10 NY jurisdictions go this far). The Board passed the original six-month moratorium unanimously after a public hearing, then noticed a 2nd-extension hearing for March 24, 2026.

The workgroup GIS sheet (updated 2026-06-18) records the moratorium as active through 9/24/26, treating this as the authoritative source. Carina Energy's June 2026 update shows Pound Ridge as LIFTED — likely aggregator lag — and the sheet's 'on-site use only' code description reflects the baseline rule that will govern once the moratorium expires. Confirm with the Town Clerk what the March 24, 2026 extension hearing formally produced (another extension vs. permanent on-site rule).
Local law: BESS only allowed when “providing energy primarily for the property on which they are located” — effectively bars utility-scale. (per the workgroup GIS sheet).
flags: Confirm directly with the Town Clerk what the March 24, 2026 hearing actually produced — whether it formally adopted the 'on-site use only' permanent rule, let the moratorium lapse without replacement, or something else. Carina's 'LIFTED' label and the workgroup's 'on-site use only' description point the same direction but neither is a primary-source confirmation.
Over-strict: On-site use only — effectively bars utility-scale storage. An engagement target to loosen toward a Croton-style tiered code.
Moratorium until 9/24/26; outside it, BESS is allowed only behind-the-meter (on-site). Not a priority per the team (limited transmission value at its location).
committees: Sustainability Committee (formerly Energy Action Committee) — key ally.
Climate Smart Communities task force — ties BESS to state climate commitments.
Conservation Board & Comprehensive Plan Update Committee — siting and land-use policy.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — future permitting bodies.
meetings: Schedule: Town Board meets the 1st & 3rd Tuesday at 7:30 PM at the Town House (179 Westchester Avenue). In person, Zoom, or livestream; agendas post ~3 days ahead.
Upcoming 2026 (7:30 PM): Jul 7, Jul 21, Aug 4.
To get on the agenda: email the Supervisor's office ≥5 days ahead and copy the Town Clerk (townclerk@townofpoundridge.gov).

towns.name: Town of Yorktown

snapshot: 90% Yellow, 10% Red — Moderate capacity (ConEd/NYSEG hosting-capacity map)
Public hearing held Oct 1, 2024 on a BESS moratorium; the moratorium is listed EXPIRED on the statewide tracker.
The Town Board passed a resolution opposing state bills S5506 / A8378 that would let ORES preempt local BESS siting.
MEDIUM — no active pause; watch for permanent regulations and the town's state-preemption stance.
summary: Yorktown enacted a BESS moratorium following an Oct 1, 2024 public hearing; it is now listed EXPIRED. The Town Board has also formally opposed S5506/A8378, signaling it wants to keep BESS siting under local control.
Confirm whether permanent regulations are in progress and engage on standards as well as the town's state-preemption advocacy.
Local law: 2025 §300-81.5: Tier 1 to 40/80 kWh, Tier 2 over 80 kWh. The Town also formally opposed state ORES preemption (S5506/A8378). (per the workgroup GIS sheet).
flags: Exact moratorium dates and any permanent-ordinance status should be confirmed via the Town Clerk / eCode360.
committees: Town Board — the legislative body; passed the moratorium and the anti-preemption resolution.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — permitting.
meetings: Schedule: Town Board regular meetings are usually the 1st & 3rd Tuesdays in the Town Hall Board Room (work sessions on additional Tuesdays). Televised on Channel 20 / YGTV live feed.

towns.name: Village of Mamaroneck

towns.subtitle: Southeastern Westchester — Long Island Sound shoreline
snapshot: Moratorium extended multiple times in 2025 (LL 7-2025, LL 11-2025, LL 14-2025) amending Chapter 342 (Zoning). The Board of Trustees held an April 27, 2026 public hearing on a proposed local law ('PLL G of 2026') addressing the BESS moratorium — the likely action that concluded the moratorium phase.
Standard safety/zoning study period; the Village Attorney presented proposals at an Oct 14, 2025 work session.
HIGH — a Tier 1-only permanent framework is now in place; push for a defined Tier 2 pathway.
summary: The Village of Mamaroneck extended its BESS moratorium repeatedly through 2025 (Local Laws 7-2025, 11-2025, and 14-2025, amending Chapter 342, Zoning) while preparing permanent regulations, then held an April 27, 2026 Board of Trustees hearing on a proposed local law specifically addressing the moratorium.

Carina Energy's June 2026 statewide update lists Mamaroneck Village's moratorium as LIFTED — consistent with the Village having concluded its drafting period and landed on a permanent Tier 1-only BESS framework rather than extending the pause again. There is now an open window to push for a defined Tier 2 pathway before the permanent code settles further.

Local law: LL 7-2026 (confirmed in workgroup GIS sheet updated 6/18/26): Tier 1 ≤40/80 kWh; >80 kWh prohibited. The moratorium chain (LL 7/11/14-2025) concluded into this permanent Tier-1-only framework after the April 27, 2026 Board of Trustees hearing. (Verified in the workgroup GIS sheet.)
flags: Confirm the current Mayor/Trustee roster + contacts on the Village site.
Over-strict: LL 7-2026 prohibits >80 kWh (Tier 2). An engagement target to loosen toward a Croton-style tiered code.
committees: Board of Trustees — Mayor + 4 Trustees; the legislative body.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — permitting.
meetings: Schedule: Board of Trustees meets the 2nd & 4th Mondays (work session 5:00 PM, regular meeting 8:00 PM) at the Village Courtroom, 169 Mt. Pleasant Avenue, Mamaroneck, NY 10543.

officials.name: Mayor + four Trustees (confirm current names/emails via the Trustees page)

No reactive ban yet — engaging early lets the workgroup help craft a sensible framework before a project triggers a hasty moratorium.
summary: Rye Brook has no BESS moratorium or ordinance on record and does not appear in the statewide tracker as of May 2026. NY's 2025 Fire Code and the State BESS Model Law are the baseline.
Best opportunity to get ahead of the issue: approach the Board of Trustees, Planning Board, and the Village's Sustainability Committee now to encourage a balanced, safety-forward ordinance.
Local law: BESS allowed, Tier 1 only. No BESS moratorium found in the workgroup data or code. (per the workgroup GIS sheet).
flags: Included in the original five, but the workgroup data shows Rye Brook PERMITS Tier 1 BESS (it has a code) and has no BESS moratorium. No separate non-BESS moratorium was found either — confirm with the boss what prompted Rye Brook's inclusion.
Over-strict: Tier 1 only — no Tier 2 pathway. An engagement target to loosen toward a Croton-style tiered code.
committees: Board of Trustees — the legislative body; any BESS law starts here.
Sustainability Committee — existing ally for a clean-energy-friendly approach.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — would review BESS site plans/variances.
meetings: Schedule: Board of Trustees meets the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of every month at Village Hall (938 King Street).

City Council adopted a 180-day moratorium (extendable another 180 days) on lithium-ion battery (and self-storage) facilities; extended in 2025.
Mayor Spano cited the need to study environmental, neighborhood and public-health impacts under SEQRA; a consultant was hired to draft regulations.
summary: At Mayor Mike Spano's request, the Yonkers City Council adopted a moratorium on lithium-ion battery facilities and extended it in 2025 while a hired consultant studies BESS regulations under SEQRA.
No BESS facilities currently operate in the city; a proposal at 1100 Saw Mill River Road is under review. Engagement runs through the Mayor and City Council.

flags: Workgroup data marks Yonkers 'code silent on BESS,' which conflicts with 2025 news of a Council moratorium. Confirm whether that moratorium lapsed or was never codified.

Carina Energy's June 2026 statewide update lists Yonkers as an ACTIVE moratorium alongside Bedford, Cortlandt, New Castle, and Peekskill — a further conflict with the 'silent' classification here. Given the contradictory signals, status is left as-is pending direct confirmation from the City Council/Mayor's office.

steps: Prioritize the towns mid-drafting — Cortlandt, New Castle, Pound Ridge, Bedford, Peekskill, Mamaroneck (Village), and Yonkers — where a permanent law is actively being written. Input now shapes the actual text.
Engage Greenburgh on its amendments. It already has a BESS law and is revising it via the Planning Board — an open, substantive process.
Get ahead in Rye Brook. With no moratorium yet, it is the best chance to help craft a balanced ordinance before a reactive ban.
Check Harrison and Yorktown's next moves. Both pauses lapsed; confirm whether permanent BESS codes are coming. Yorktown is also fighting state preemption (S5506/A8378).
Study Port Chester as a model. It chose permanent regulations over a ban — useful precedent and language for other towns.
Monitor the ban towns (Mount Pleasant, Mount Kisco). Bans can be revisited or challenged; watch for reconsideration or litigation.
Get on agendas in advance. Most boards require contacting the clerk/supervisor days ahead (Pound Ridge: 5 days, copy the Clerk). Public-comment periods are also available at each meeting.
Lead with the sustainability committees in each town for a “safe siting, not outright ban” message, and track the state bill S5506 / A8378.
sources: Carina Energy — NY BESS Moratoriums tracker (May 2026)
Town of Cortlandt — LL 2 of 2025 (moratorium extension)
Mamaroneck Village — Recently Adopted Local Laws 2025
Port Chester — Board of Trustees / Agenda Center
Mount Pleasant — press release: BESS ban
The Examiner — Mount Kisco BESS moratorium/ban

items: Cortlandt CORRECTION (major): LL 7-2025 confirmed to extend the moratorium through Dec 31, 2026 — not July 31, 2026 as the dashboard previously showed. Source: the Town Board's public hearing notice for December 9, 2025 (fetched from the Town's legal notices page 6/30/26) states the extension runs 'through the end of 2026.' The workgroup GIS sheet notation 'until 07-2026' was likely a date-entry error; the legal notice text controls. Updated scope, governing-law snapshot, summary, flags, calendar deadline, and priority entry accordingly. Added Nov 17 and Dec 8 Town Board meetings to the calendar.

Mamaroneck Village: confirmed adopted law is LL 7-2026 (Tier 1 ≤40/80 kWh; >80 kWh prohibited) per the workgroup GIS sheet (updated 2026-06-18). Removed the 'unconfirmed law number' flag and updated scope and summary to reflect the confirmed law. Consistent with the April 27, 2026 Board of Trustees hearing on 'PLL G of 2026' that concluded the moratorium chain.

Pound Ridge: fixed summary inconsistency — previously said the moratorium 'appears to have given way,' but the workgroup GIS sheet (authoritative) shows moratorium active through 9/24/26. Summary rewritten to clearly state the workgroup source is being treated as controlling over Carina's 'LIFTED' label.

Carina Energy tracker and workgroup GIS spreadsheet (last updated 2026-06-18) checked — no new Westchester municipalities adopted BESS rules or new public hearings surfaced since 6/29/26. NY S5506/A8378 unchanged, still in committee.

New Castle: confirmed via Carina Energy's June 2026 update that the moratorium remains ACTIVE through roughly August 2026 (not lapsed 5/31/26 as the workgroup spreadsheet suggested); the Town's online Approved Legislation page still shows no 2026 entries, so the exact extension law is unconfirmed — flagged as a possible legal gap pending direct Town Clerk confirmation. Also found the proposed permanent law has shifted toward prohibiting Tier 2/3 outright (Tier 1 ≤80 kWh only), a stricter direction than previously described; Planning Board responded to the referral March 4, 2026, Town Board vote still pending.

Mamaroneck Village: found a April 27, 2026 Board of Trustees hearing on a proposed local law ('PLL G of 2026') addressing the BESS moratorium — not previously in this dataset. Resolved the prior internal inconsistency (status field said 'overstrict' while flags said 'Status kept ACTIVE'): the moratorium chain (LL 7/11/14-2025) appears to have concluded into a permanent Tier-1-only framework, consistent with Carina's June 2026 'LIFTED' label. Status unchanged (overstrict); flags/summary rewritten for consistency. Exact adopted law number still unconfirmed.

Pound Ridge: similarly resolved a stale claim that the March 24, 2026 hearing 'extended the moratorium, so it remains active' — Carina's June 2026 update lists Pound Ridge as LIFTED, consistent with the workgroup's own 'on-site use only' description of the town's current rule. Status unchanged (overstrict); summary/flags rewritten to stop asserting the moratorium is still active.
Village of Ossining: enriched with Local Law 4-2026's 8-month term (effective ~4/22/26), implying a computed (unconfirmed) expiration around late December 2026.

Harrison: enriched with the specific tiered standard now in force, Town Code §235-95 (Tier 1 ≤80 kWh and Tier 2 ≤600 kWh permitted outright; Tier 3 >600 kWh via Special Permit Use) — updated the action-plan ask accordingly (push on Tier 3 permit criteria, not on creating tiers).

Corrected a stale state.points claim that the coalition tracks '7 active moratorium towns' — the actual current count is 4 (Bedford, Cortlandt, New Castle, Village of Ossining), with Mamaroneck Village and Pound Ridge now under permanent restrictive codes rather than active moratoriums.

Added flags to Peekskill and Yonkers noting Carina's June 2026 update lists both as ACTIVE moratoriums, in tension with this dataset's 'lapsed 5/31/26' and 'silent' characterizations respectively — left unchanged pending direct confirmation.

Re-pulled the Carina Energy NY tracker (June 2026 update) and the Google Drive 'ArcGIS data BESS 2026' workgroup spreadsheet; reviewed all ~45 towns — no other discrepancies found. NY S5506/A8378 unchanged, still in committee.

Carina Energy NY tracker refreshed June 26, 2026 reclassified three Westchester towns: Mamaroneck Village and Pound Ridge from ACTIVE to LIFTED, and Mount Pleasant (BAN) to LIFTED — dropping its Westchester active count from 8 to 5.

Verified against official town sources: Pound Ridge noticed a 2nd-extension public hearing for its BESS moratorium on March 24, 2026, and Mamaroneck Village extended its moratorium via Local Law 14-2025 (Chapter 342). Both moratoriums remain ACTIVE — statuses NOT changed; Carina's 'lifted' labels treated as aggregator lag.
Mount Pleasant kept as permanent BAN; Carina's 'lifted moratorium' is consistent with the Dec 2025 prohibition law having superseded the prior moratorium.
NY S5506 (Kavanagh) / A8378 (Levenberg) ORES standalone-storage expansion — still in the Energy & Telecommunications Committee as of June 2026; not passed.
No new Westchester municipality adopted a BESS rule; no new public hearing/vote surfaced for the other monitored towns (Cortlandt, New Castle, Bedford, Peekskill, Yonkers).
Re-confirmed every town's status against the Carina Energy NY tracker, which was refreshed June 14, 2026 (now 104 active NY moratoriums statewide; Westchester tied for the most with 8).

Tracker cross-check for the 8 Westchester active-moratorium towns held: Cortlandt, New Castle, Pound Ridge (ALL_BESS), Bedford, Peekskill, Mamaroneck (Village), Yonkers — all ACTIVE; Harrison and Yorktown remain EXPIRED; Port Chester remains off the moratorium list (regulated).

Noted tracker nuances: the moratorium tracker now lists Mount Pleasant as ACTIVE and Mount Kisco as EXPIRED, while this dashboard reports each as an effective BAN based on their own town actions (Mount Pleasant's Dec 2025 prohibition law; Mount Kisco's Aug 2025 'not a public utility' interpretation). Statuses kept as bans; see flags.
NY S5506 (Kavanagh) / A8378 (Levenberg) — ORES standalone-storage expansion — still in the Energy & Telecommunications Committee; not passed either chamber.
No new Westchester municipality appeared on the tracker; no new public BESS hearing/vote surfaced for any monitored town since the prior run.
Baseline dashboard build prior to daily-refresh changelog tracking.

deadlines.label: Cortlandt — BESS moratorium expires 12/31/26 per LL 7-2025 (engage before year-end)

priority.name: Town of Cortlandt (Cortlandt Manor)
priority.why: Active moratorium on ALL BESS expires 12/31/26 (LL 7-2025); large town; High grid capacity (100% yellow). Next Town Board: July 21, 2026.
priority.action: Engage Town Board now — push a Croton-style tiered code so permanent rules allow Tier 2+ rather than defaulting to the current Tier 3 prohibition.

priority.name: Town of New Castle (Chappaqua / Millwood)
priority.why: Moratorium lapsed 5/31/26; tiered law proposed but not adopted — open window now; Moderate capacity.
priority.action: Comment at Town Board to shape thresholds toward a permissive Tier 1 and reasonable Tier 3.

priority.name: Town / Village of Harrison
priority.why: Moratorium expired; code already allows to Tier 3 by permit; big commercial base; High capacity — high impact, low friction.
priority.action: Lock in / formalize the tiered standards and build relationships before any reactive rollback.

priority.name: City of Peekskill
priority.why: Tier 1 only with the Tier 2 moratorium just lapsed (5/31/26) — clear opening; Hudson city; High capacity.
priority.action: Advocate a Tier 2 standard at Common Council now.

priority.name: Town of Bedford
priority.why: Large-scale moratorium until 8/17/26 (urgent deadline), but Low grid capacity (100% red) tempers payoff.
priority.action: Engage before 8/17/26 to add a Tier 2 pathway; weigh effort vs. limited grid value.

priority.name: Village of Rye Brook
priority.why: Restrictive (Tier 1 only); High capacity; flagged by the boss among the original five.
priority.action: Confirm why Rye Brook was flagged, then push a Tier 2 pathway.

priority.name: Village of Mamaroneck
priority.action: Advocate raising the cap / adding a Tier 2 pathway.

priority.name: Village of Ossining
priority.why: Fresh 8-month moratorium (ends ~Dec 2026) — good timing, but Low grid capacity (100% red) limits impact.
priority.action: Engage the Village Board to steer toward 'allowed,' but prioritize higher-capacity towns first.

priority.name: Village of Briarcliff Manor
priority.why: Restrictive — prohibits Tier 2 (>600 kWh); Low capacity (75% red).
priority.action: Push to allow Tier 2 with siting standards when capacity allows.

priority.name: Village of Larchmont
priority.why: Restrictive — LL 2-2026 prohibits Tier 2 (>600 kWh); High grid capacity (100% yellow).
priority.action: Push to add a Tier 2 pathway with siting/safety standards.

hearingNote: Specific public-hearing dates are not centrally published — towns post them as one-off legal notices (often via eCode360 or the town-clerk page). Recurring board meetings below are computed; confirm exact hearing dates per town. Gaps to research: firm 2026 hearing dates for Cortlandt, New Castle, and Ossining Village.
hostingNote: Grid hosting capacity is a proxy read from the ConEd hosting-capacity map colors in the workgroup sheet: more green/yellow = more available capacity (better BESS siting value); orange = limited; red = constrained. NYSEG-territory towns aren't on that map. Confirm against the utility legend before relying on it.
